# =============================================================================
#  .github/workflows/generate-llm-docs.yml
#
#  OpenWrt LLM Documentation Pipeline — v5
#  Implements: openwrt-llm-pipeline-techspec-v5.md
#
#  TWO-TIER ARCHITECTURE:
#    Tier 1 — This workflow (automated, monthly, $0):
#      Clones upstream repos, generates API docs, scrapes wiki (incrementally),
#      extracts examples, injects cross-references, checks deprecations, runs
#      AI summaries, assembles single-file references, publishes to GitHub Pages.
#
#    Tier 2 — User-maintained (local, updated rarely):
#      static-docs/SYSTEM-PROMPT.md, concepts-glossary.md, openwrt-recipes.md
#      Created locally with a capable AI, reviewed, then uploaded manually.
#      This workflow copies them to the Pages root unchanged.
#
#  OUTPUT FILE STRUCTURE:
#    ucode-docs/                    Per-module ucode API reference + llms.txt
#    luci-docs/                     Per-class LuCI JS API reference + llms.txt
#    openwrt-wiki-docs/             Wiki pages (last 2 years, incremental) + llms.txt
#    openwrt-buildroot-docs/        Package metadata from buildroot + llms.txt
#    openwrt-examples/              4 curated LuCI app examples + llms.txt
#    *-complete-reference.md        Standalone single-file versions for direct LLM use
#    llms.txt                       Root index (llmstxt.org standard)
#    CHANGES.md                     Diff of this run vs. previous (new in v5)
#    SYSTEM-PROMPT.md               } Promoted from static-docs/ if present
#    concepts-glossary.md           }
#    openwrt-recipes.md             }
#
#  CHANGES FROM v4:
#    - Step 1: Removed unreliable live-site jsdoc config scraping; each step
#      now searches for config files directly in the cloned repo (reliable)
#    - Steps 2a/b/c: Fatal || { echo; exit 1; } on all git clone commands
#    - Step 2c: Added package/firmware, package/boot to buildroot sparse checkout
#    - Step 3/4: jsdoc2md stderr logged to named file, reported after processing
#    - Step 5: Wiki scraping now incremental (unchanged pages skipped);
#      sitemap failures now graceful exit(0) instead of fatal exit(1)
#    - Step 7: App llms.txt now includes a file tree view per app
#    - Step 8: Symbol filter now excludes common English words and requires
#      code-like names (camelCase, dotted.name, or underscore_style)
#    - Step 9: Deprecated symbol search window increased 300 → 1500 chars
#    - Step 10: Correct GitHub Models endpoint (models.github.ai) and model
#      identifier (openai/gpt-4o-mini); safe .get() on API response
#    - Steps 3/4/8/9/10: All file writes use with open() context managers
#    - Steps 11a-11e: Complete reference files have standalone-use header note
#    - Step 12a (new): CHANGES.md diff document generated each run
#    - Step 12b (new): Output validation — fails workflow if critical outputs
#      are suspiciously small, catching silent generation failures
#    - Step 13: upload-pages-artifact updated from v3 to v4
#    - Step 14: git add --all tracks deletions; git push is non-fatal
#    - Summary: fixed unquoted variable in ls command
#
#  ONE-TIME REPO SETUP REQUIRED:
#    Settings → Pages → Source → select "GitHub Actions"
# =============================================================================

name: Generate LLM-Optimized Documentation

on:
  schedule:
    # Monthly on the 1st at 02:00 UTC.
    # OpenWrt docs change slowly; monthly is polite to openwrt.org servers
    # and aligns with the pace of meaningful upstream changes.
    - cron: '0 2 1 * *'

  workflow_dispatch:
    # Manual "Run Workflow" button with step-skipping checkboxes.
    # Use during development to skip slow steps.
    inputs:
      skip_wiki:
        description: 'Skip OpenWrt wiki scraping (faster run)'
        required: false
        default: 'false'
        type: choice
        options: ['false', 'true']
      skip_buildroot:
        description: 'Skip buildroot source extraction (faster run)'
        required: false
        default: 'false'
        type: choice
        options: ['false', 'true']
      skip_ai:
        description: 'Skip AI summarization (saves GitHub Models quota)'
        required: false
        default: 'false'
        type: choice
        options: ['false', 'true']

# Permissions required:
#   contents: write  — commit generated files back to the repo
#   pages: write     — deploy to GitHub Pages
#   id-token: write  — OIDC auth required by actions/deploy-pages
permissions:
  contents: write
  pages: write
  id-token: write

# Prevent overlapping runs. A new manual trigger cancels any in-progress run.
concurrency:
  group: generate-llm-docs
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:

  # ===========================================================================
  #  JOB 1: generate-docs
  #  Does all generation work. Uploads result as a GitHub Pages artifact.
  # ===========================================================================
  generate-docs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60

    steps:

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  SETUP
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: "Setup: Checkout repository"
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Setup: Node.js 20"
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: "Setup: Python 3.12"
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: "Setup: Install pandoc"
        # pandoc is chosen over html2text because it has a native DokuWiki
        # input format (-f dokuwiki), producing cleaner GFM output. html2text
        # only works on rendered HTML and produces more noise.
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update -qq
          sudo apt-get install -y pandoc

      - name: "Setup: Install jsdoc-to-markdown"
        # The same tool used by upstream ucode and LuCI developers to build
        # their live documentation sites. Per-file invocation gives us
        # granular named output files rather than one merged blob.
        run: npm install -g jsdoc-to-markdown

      - name: "Setup: Install Python packages"
        run: pip install requests beautifulsoup4 lxml --quiet

      - name: "Setup: Prepare output directories"
        # Clear stale .md files from previous runs to prevent accumulating
        # artifacts from renamed or removed upstream pages.
        run: |
          mkdir -p ucode-docs luci-docs openwrt-wiki-docs \
                   openwrt-buildroot-docs openwrt-examples

          rm -f ucode-docs/*.md ucode-docs/llms.txt
          rm -f luci-docs/*.md luci-docs/llms.txt
          rm -f openwrt-wiki-docs/*.md openwrt-wiki-docs/llms.txt
          rm -f openwrt-buildroot-docs/*.md openwrt-buildroot-docs/llms.txt
          rm -f openwrt-examples/llms.txt

          rm -f ucode-complete-reference.md
          rm -f luci-jsapi-complete-reference.md
          rm -f openwrt-wiki-complete-reference.md
          rm -f openwrt-buildroot-complete-reference.md
          rm -f openwrt-examples-complete-reference.md
          rm -f llms.txt CHANGES.md

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 1: Note on jsdoc config detection
      #
      #  v4 attempted to detect the jsdoc config file path by scraping the live
      #  documentation websites. This approach does not work in practice because
      #  jsdoc does not embed the config path in its HTML output — it is only
      #  used at build time and leaves no trace in the generated site.
      #
      #  v5 replaces this with a simple post-clone search inside each repo
      #  directory (see Steps 3 and 4). Each step searches for common config
      #  file names in the cloned repo and uses the first one found. This is
      #  simple, reliable, and requires no network requests.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 1: Config detection is post-clone (see Steps 3 and 4)"
        run: |
          echo "jsdoc config detection: each doc-generation step searches the"
          echo "cloned repo for common config filenames after cloning."
          echo "Checked in order: jsdoc/conf.json, jsdoc.conf.json, .jsdoc.json"

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 2: Clone upstream repositories
      #
      #  ucode + luci: shallow clone (small repos, depth=1 is sufficient)
      #  openwrt:      sparse checkout — full repo is multi-gigabyte; we only
      #                need package/, include/, and scripts/
      #
      #  ERROR HANDLING: All git clone commands use || { echo; exit 1; } because
      #  clone failures are FATAL — no downstream step can produce output without
      #  the source repos. This is different from wiki scraping failures, which
      #  are non-fatal because they are optional external data sources.
      #
      #  Commit hashes are recorded to $GITHUB_ENV for traceability in file
      #  metadata headers and the final commit message.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 2a: Clone ucode (jow-/ucode)"
        run: |
          git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/jow-/ucode.git repo-ucode \
            || { echo "ERROR: Failed to clone ucode repo"; exit 1; }
          echo "UCODE_COMMIT=$(git -C repo-ucode rev-parse --short HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "Cloned ucode @ $(git -C repo-ucode rev-parse --short HEAD)"

      - name: "Step 2b: Clone LuCI (openwrt/luci)"
        run: |
          git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/openwrt/luci.git repo-luci \
            || { echo "ERROR: Failed to clone luci repo"; exit 1; }
          echo "LUCI_COMMIT=$(git -C repo-luci rev-parse --short HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "Cloned luci @ $(git -C repo-luci rev-parse --short HEAD)"

      - name: "Step 2c: Clone OpenWrt buildroot (sparse checkout)"
        if: ${{ github.event.inputs.skip_buildroot != 'true' }}
        run: |
          # --filter=blob:none: don't download file blobs until checked out
          # --sparse: start with empty working tree; we set what we need below
          # This avoids downloading gigabytes of kernel patches and toolchain.
          git clone --depth=1 --filter=blob:none --sparse \
            https://github.com/openwrt/openwrt.git repo-openwrt \
            || { echo "ERROR: Failed to clone openwrt repo"; exit 1; }

          cd repo-openwrt
          git sparse-checkout set \
            package/network \
            package/kernel \
            package/utils \
            package/system \
            package/libs \
            package/firmware \
            package/boot \
            package/multimedia \
            include \
            scripts
          echo "OPENWRT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "Cloned openwrt @ $(git rev-parse --short HEAD) (sparse)"
          cd ..

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 3: Generate ucode documentation
      #
      #  Two content types:
      #    Tutorials: docs/tutorial-*.md — already markdown; copy with header.
      #    Modules:   lib/*.js, lib/*.c  — JSDoc annotations; run jsdoc2md.
      #
      #  JSDOC CONFIG: Search for config file in common locations inside the
      #  cloned repo. Use --configure only if a config file is found.
      #  jsdoc2md works correctly without a config file; this is just an
      #  optimization to use the upstream developer's own settings.
      #
      #  STDERR LOGGING: jsdoc2md errors go to ../jsdoc-ucode.err (not /dev/null)
      #  so problems are visible when a module produces unexpectedly empty output.
      #
      #  HEREDOC NOTE: Content lines of the llms.txt heredoc must be at the
      #  same indentation level as all other run: block content (10 spaces).
      #  The YAML parser strips this common indent, leaving content at column 0
      #  in the shell script where the heredoc terminator is recognized correctly.
      #
      #  Single-file assembly is done in Step 11, AFTER cross-reference injection
      #  (Step 8), so both modular files and complete references receive links.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 3: Generate ucode documentation"
        run: |
          cd repo-ucode
          npm install --silent || { echo "ERROR: npm install failed in repo-ucode"; exit 1; }

          OUT="../ucode-docs"
          TS=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          REPO_URL="https://github.com/jow-/ucode"

          # Search for jsdoc config in common locations within the cloned repo.
          # This replaces the v4 approach of scraping the live site (which did
          # not work — jsdoc does not embed the config path in HTML output).
          CONF_ARG=""
          for conf in jsdoc/conf.json jsdoc.conf.json .jsdoc.json .jsdoc.conf.json; do
            if [ -f "$conf" ]; then
              CONF_ARG="--configure $conf"
              echo "Found jsdoc config: $conf"
              break
            fi
          done
          [ -z "$CONF_ARG" ] && echo "No jsdoc config found; running without --configure"

          # Initialize per-folder llms.txt.
          # HEREDOC INDENTATION: content lines must be at 10 spaces in the YAML
          # so the YAML parser's common-indent strip leaves them at column 0,
          # where the shell can find the LLMS_EOF closing delimiter correctly.
          cat > "$OUT/llms.txt" << LLMS_EOF
# ucode Documentation Index
# Source: $REPO_URL (commit: $UCODE_COMMIT)
# Live docs: https://ucode.mein.io/
# Generated: $TS
#
# ucode is a tiny ECMAScript-like scripting language for OpenWrt.
# Provides bindings for ubus, uci, uloop, netlink, and other OpenWrt APIs.
# Syntax closely resembles ECMAScript; synchronous; no OOP standard library.
#
# Standalone use: each .md file in this folder is self-contained with full
# metadata headers. This llms.txt provides the navigational index.
#
## Files in this directory

LLMS_EOF

          # ── Tutorials: copy from docs/tutorial-*.md with metadata header ──
          echo "Processing tutorials..."
          for src in docs/tutorial-*.md; do
            [ -f "$src" ] || continue

            base=$(basename "$src")
            dest=$(echo "$base" | sed 's/tutorial-[0-9]*-/ucode-tutorial-/')
            title=$(grep -m1 "^#" "$src" | sed 's/^#* *//')

            {
              echo "# $title"
              echo ""
              echo "> **Source:** [\`$src\`]($REPO_URL/blob/master/$src)"
              echo "> **Live docs:** https://ucode.mein.io/$(basename "$src" .md).html"
              echo "> **Generated:** $TS from commit \`$UCODE_COMMIT\`"
              echo ""
              echo "---"
              echo ""
              tail -n +2 "$src"
            } > "$OUT/$dest"

            echo "- [$dest](/ucode-docs/$dest): ucode tutorial — $title" >> "$OUT/llms.txt"
            echo "  Tutorial: $dest"
          done

          # ── Modules: jsdoc2md per source file ──
          echo ""
          echo "Processing modules..."
          for src in lib/*.js lib/*.c; do
            [ -f "$src" ] || continue

            mod=$(basename "$src" | sed 's/\.[^.]*$//')

            # Stderr to named file — not /dev/null — so we can report problems.
            # The || true prevents a non-zero exit from jsdoc2md killing the loop.
            OUTPUT=$(jsdoc2md $CONF_ARG \
              --heading-depth 2 \
              --global-index-format none \
              "$src" 2>>"../jsdoc-ucode.err" || true)

            word_count=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | wc -w)
            if [ -z "$OUTPUT" ] || [ "$word_count" -lt 15 ]; then
              echo "  Skipping $src ($word_count words — below threshold)"
              continue
            fi

            outfile="$OUT/ucode-module-${mod}.md"
            member_count=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c "^##" || echo "0")

            {
              echo "# ucode module: \`$mod\`"
              echo ""
              echo "> **Source:** [\`$src\`]($REPO_URL/blob/master/$src)"
              echo "> **Live docs:** https://ucode.mein.io/module-${mod}.html"
              echo "> **Generated:** $TS from commit \`$UCODE_COMMIT\`"
              echo ""
              echo "---"
              echo ""
              echo "$OUTPUT"
            } > "$outfile"

            echo "- [ucode-module-${mod}.md](/ucode-docs/ucode-module-${mod}.md): ucode \`$mod\` module — $member_count documented members" \
              >> "$OUT/llms.txt"
            echo "  Module: ucode-module-${mod}.md ($member_count members)"
          done

          cd ..

          # Report any jsdoc warnings so they are visible in the Actions log.
          if [ -s jsdoc-ucode.err ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "=== jsdoc warnings/errors (ucode) ==="
            cat jsdoc-ucode.err
            echo "=== end jsdoc warnings ==="
          fi
          rm -f jsdoc-ucode.err

          echo "Step 3 complete."

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 4: Generate LuCI JS API documentation
      #
      #  Source: modules/luci-base/htdocs/luci-static/resources/
      #  Same config detection and stderr logging approach as Step 3.
      #
      #  Live doc URL convention:
      #    Top-level luci class: https://openwrt.github.io/luci/jsapi/LuCI.html
      #    Other modules:        https://openwrt.github.io/luci/jsapi/LuCI.{mod}.html
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 4: Generate LuCI JS API documentation"
        run: |
          cd repo-luci
          TARGET="modules/luci-base/htdocs/luci-static/resources"
          OUT="../luci-docs"
          TS=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          REPO_URL="https://github.com/openwrt/luci"
          LIVE_BASE="https://openwrt.github.io/luci/jsapi"

          # Same config detection approach as Step 3.
          CONF_ARG=""
          for conf in jsdoc.conf.json jsdoc/conf.json .jsdoc.json .jsdoc.conf.json; do
            if [ -f "$conf" ]; then
              CONF_ARG="--configure $conf"
              echo "Found jsdoc config: $conf"
              break
            fi
          done
          [ -z "$CONF_ARG" ] && echo "No jsdoc config found; running without --configure"

          cat > "$OUT/llms.txt" << LLMS_EOF
# LuCI JS API Documentation Index
# Source: $REPO_URL (commit: $LUCI_COMMIT)
# Live docs: $LIVE_BASE/
# Generated: $TS
#
# LuCI is the OpenWrt web configuration interface.
# This index covers its complete client-side JavaScript API.
# Includes: DOM, UCI, RPC, network info, UI widgets, forms, request handling.
#
# Standalone use: each .md file in this folder is self-contained with full
# metadata headers. This llms.txt provides the navigational index.
#
## Files in this directory

LLMS_EOF

          echo "Processing LuCI JS API source files..."
          file_count=0
          skip_count=0

          # sort -z ensures consistent alphabetical output ordering across runs.
          while IFS= read -r -d '' src; do
            filename=$(basename "$src")
            mod="${filename%.*}"
            relpath="${src#./}"

            OUTPUT=$(jsdoc2md $CONF_ARG \
              --heading-depth 2 \
              --global-index-format none \
              "$src" 2>>"../jsdoc-luci.err" || true)

            word_count=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | wc -w)
            if [ -z "$OUTPUT" ] || [ "$word_count" -lt 15 ]; then
              skip_count=$((skip_count + 1))
              continue
            fi

            if [ "$mod" = "luci" ]; then
              live_url="$LIVE_BASE/LuCI.html"
            else
              live_url="$LIVE_BASE/LuCI.${mod}.html"
            fi

            outfile="$OUT/luci-api-${mod}.md"
            member_count=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c "^##" || echo "0")

            {
              echo "# LuCI API: \`$mod\`"
              echo ""
              echo "> **Source:** [\`$relpath\`]($REPO_URL/blob/master/$relpath)"
              echo "> **Live docs:** $live_url"
              echo "> **Generated:** $TS from commit \`$LUCI_COMMIT\`"
              echo ""
              echo "---"
              echo ""
              echo "$OUTPUT"
            } > "$outfile"

            echo "- [luci-api-${mod}.md](/luci-docs/luci-api-${mod}.md): LuCI \`$mod\` JS API — $member_count documented members" \
              >> "$OUT/llms.txt"
            echo "  API: luci-api-${mod}.md ($member_count members)"
            file_count=$((file_count + 1))

          done < <(find "$TARGET" -name "*.js" -print0 | sort -z)

          cd ..

          if [ -s jsdoc-luci.err ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "=== jsdoc warnings/errors (LuCI) ==="
            cat jsdoc-luci.err
            echo "=== end jsdoc warnings ==="
          fi
          rm -f jsdoc-luci.err

          echo "Step 4 complete: $file_count files generated, $skip_count skipped."

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 5: Scrape OpenWrt wiki (incremental, last 2 years)
      #
      #  DISCOVERY STRATEGY — sitemap-first:
      #    One HTTP request to sitemap.xml gives lastmod for every page.
      #    Age filter applied before fetching any page content.
      #
      #  INCREMENTAL SCRAPING (new in v5):
      #    For each candidate page, check whether an existing output file
      #    already has a Last modified date matching the sitemap. If so,
      #    skip the fetch and reuse the existing file. This reduces server
      #    load and speeds up monthly runs significantly.
      #
      #  NON-FATAL STEP: sitemap fetch/parse failures use exit(0), not exit(1).
      #  If openwrt.org is temporarily down, the workflow continues and produces
      #  all other outputs. The wiki output folder simply remains empty.
      #
      #  CONVERSION: ?do=export_raw returns raw DokuWiki markup.
      #  pandoc -f dokuwiki converts natively without HTML rendering artifacts.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 5: Scrape OpenWrt wiki (incremental, last 2 years)"
        if: ${{ github.event.inputs.skip_wiki != 'true' }}
        run: |
          python3 << 'PYEOF'
          import os
          import re
          import time
          import datetime
          import subprocess
          import requests
          from xml.etree import ElementTree
          from urllib.parse import urlparse

          OUT_DIR = "openwrt-wiki-docs"
          DELAY   = 1.5   # seconds between requests — polite to openwrt.org
          CUTOFF  = datetime.datetime.utcnow() - datetime.timedelta(days=730)

          INCLUDE_PREFIXES = [
              "/docs/techref/",
              "/docs/guide-developer/",
          ]

          SKIP_PATTERNS = [
              "/toh/",
              "/inbox/",
              "/meta/",
              "/playground/",
              "changelog",
              "release_notes",
          ]

          session = requests.Session()
          session.headers.update({
              "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (openwrt-llm-docs-bot/1.0; https://github.com)"
          })

          # ── Incremental: read last-modified date from an existing output file ──
          def get_existing_lastmod(fname):
              """
              Read the Last modified date from the metadata header of an
              existing output file. Returns datetime or None.
              Header format:  > **Last modified:** YYYY-MM-DD
              """
              fpath = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, fname)
              if not os.path.isfile(fpath):
                  return None
              try:
                  with open(fpath, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
                      for line in fh:
                          m = re.search(r'\*\*Last modified:\*\*\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})', line)
                          if m:
                              return datetime.datetime.strptime(m.group(1), "%Y-%m-%d")
                          if line.startswith("---"):
                              break
              except Exception:
                  pass
              return None

          def get_existing_title(fname):
              """Read the H1 title from an existing output file."""
              fpath = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, fname)
              try:
                  with open(fpath, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
                      first = fh.readline().strip()
                      if first.startswith("# "):
                          return first[2:]
              except Exception:
                  pass
              return fname

          # ── Phase 1: Parse sitemap ─────────────────────────────────────────────
          print("Fetching https://openwrt.org/sitemap.xml ...")
          try:
              resp = session.get("https://openwrt.org/sitemap.xml", timeout=30)
              resp.raise_for_status()
          except Exception as e:
              # NON-FATAL: wiki is optional. exit(0) lets the workflow continue.
              print(f"WARNING: Could not fetch sitemap: {e}")
              print("Skipping wiki documentation generation.")
              exit(0)

          NS = {"sm": "http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"}
          try:
              sitemap_root = ElementTree.fromstring(resp.content)
          except Exception as e:
              # NON-FATAL: malformed sitemap — skip wiki gracefully.
              print(f"WARNING: Could not parse sitemap XML: {e}")
              print("Skipping wiki documentation generation.")
              exit(0)

          candidates = []
          total_in_sitemap = 0

          for url_el in sitemap_root.findall("sm:url", NS):
              total_in_sitemap += 1
              loc_el = url_el.find("sm:loc",     NS)
              mod_el = url_el.find("sm:lastmod", NS)
              if loc_el is None:
                  continue

              url  = loc_el.text.strip()
              path = urlparse(url).path

              if not any(path.startswith(p) for p in INCLUDE_PREFIXES):
                  continue
              if any(pat in path for pat in SKIP_PATTERNS):
                  continue

              last_mod = None
              if mod_el is not None and mod_el.text:
                  try:
                      last_mod = datetime.datetime.strptime(
                          mod_el.text.strip()[:10], "%Y-%m-%d"
                      )
                  except ValueError:
                      pass

              if last_mod is not None and last_mod < CUTOFF:
                  continue

              candidates.append((url, path, last_mod))

          print(f"Sitemap: {total_in_sitemap} total pages")
          print(f"After scope/age filter: {len(candidates)} candidates")

          # ── Phase 2: Fetch and convert ────────────────────────────────────────
          def path_to_filename(url_path):
              parts = url_path.strip("/").split("/")
              if parts and parts[0] == "docs":
                  parts = parts[1:]
              if parts and parts[-1] == "start":
                  parts = parts[:-1]
              slug = "-".join(p for p in parts if p)
              slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9-]", "-", slug.lower())
              slug = re.sub(r"-+", "-", slug).strip("-")
              return f"openwrt-{slug}.md" if slug else "openwrt-misc.md"

          toc_entries = []
          saved = 0
          skipped_unchanged = 0
          failed = 0

          for url, path, last_mod in candidates:
              fname = path_to_filename(path)

              # ── Incremental check: skip if existing file is already up to date ──
              existing_mod = get_existing_lastmod(fname)
              if existing_mod and last_mod and existing_mod >= last_mod:
                  title = get_existing_title(fname)
                  toc_entries.append((fname, title, url, last_mod.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")))
                  skipped_unchanged += 1
                  continue

              time.sleep(DELAY)

              raw_url = url + "?do=export_raw"
              try:
                  r = session.get(raw_url, timeout=20)
                  r.raise_for_status()
                  raw_content = r.text
              except Exception as e:
                  print(f"  FAIL ({e}): {path}")
                  failed += 1
                  continue

              if not raw_content.strip():
                  continue

              try:
                  result = subprocess.run(
                      ["pandoc", "-f", "dokuwiki", "-t", "gfm", "--wrap=none"],
                      input=raw_content, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30
                  )
                  md = result.stdout
              except Exception as e:
                  print(f"  PANDOC FAIL ({e}): {path}")
                  failed += 1
                  continue

              md = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", md).strip()

              if len(md) < 200:
                  continue

              date_str  = last_mod.strftime("%Y-%m-%d") if last_mod else "unknown"
              fetch_ts  = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
              title_m   = re.search(r"^#+ (.+)$", md, re.MULTILINE)
              title     = title_m.group(1).strip() if title_m else path.split("/")[-1]

              with open(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, fname), "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                  f.write(f"# {title}\n\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Source:** {url}\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Last modified:** {date_str}\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Fetched:** {fetch_ts}\n\n")
                  f.write("---\n\n")
                  f.write(re.sub(r"^#+ .+\n\n?", "", md, count=1))

              toc_entries.append((fname, title, url, date_str))
              saved += 1
              print(f"  OK [{date_str}] {fname} — {title[:55]}")

          ts = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          with open(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "llms.txt"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
              f.write("# OpenWrt Wiki Developer Documentation Index\n")
              f.write("# Source: https://openwrt.org/docs/\n")
              f.write(f"# Generated: {ts}\n")
              f.write("# Scope: /docs/techref/ + /docs/guide-developer/ — last 2 years\n")
              f.write("# Incremental: unchanged pages are preserved from previous run.\n")
              f.write("#\n## Files in this directory\n\n")
              for fname, title, url, date in sorted(toc_entries):
                  f.write(
                      f"- [{fname}](/openwrt-wiki-docs/{fname}): "
                      f"{title} (modified: {date}) — {url}\n"
                  )

          print(
              f"\nStep 5 complete: {saved} fetched, "
              f"{skipped_unchanged} unchanged/cached, {failed} failed."
          )
          PYEOF

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 6: Extract OpenWrt buildroot documentation
      #
      #  Extracts:
      #    - Makefile fields: PKG_DESCRIPTION, PKG_VERSION, PKG_LICENSE,
      #      PKG_MAINTAINER, PKG_SOURCE_URL
      #    - README files (capped at 2000 chars)
      #    - include/*.mk header comment blocks
      #
      #  Output: one .md per top-level package category + include-mk.md
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 6: Extract OpenWrt buildroot documentation"
        if: ${{ github.event.inputs.skip_buildroot != 'true' }}
        run: |
          python3 << 'PYEOF'
          import os
          import re
          import glob
          import datetime

          REPO     = "repo-openwrt"
          OUT_DIR  = "openwrt-buildroot-docs"
          TS       = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          COMMIT   = os.environ.get("OPENWRT_COMMIT", "unknown")
          REPO_URL = "https://github.com/openwrt/openwrt"

          if not os.path.isdir(REPO):
              print("Buildroot repo not found (step skipped or clone failed).")
              exit(0)

          def extract_makefile_meta(path):
              try:
                  text = open(path, encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").read()
              except Exception:
                  return {}
              fields = {}
              for key in ["PKG_NAME", "PKG_VERSION", "PKG_SOURCE_URL",
                          "PKG_MAINTAINER", "PKG_LICENSE"]:
                  m = re.search(
                      rf'^{key}\s*[?:+]?=\s*(.+?)(?=\n[A-Z_]|\Z)',
                      text, re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL
                  )
                  if m:
                      val = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", m.group(1).replace("\\\n", " ")).strip()
                      if val and not val.startswith("$("):
                          fields[key] = val[:200]

              m = re.search(
                  r'^PKG_DESCRIPTION\s*[?:+]?=\s*(.+?)(?=\n[A-Z]|\Z)',
                  text, re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL
              )
              if m:
                  fields["DESCRIPTION"] = re.sub(
                      r"\s+", " ", m.group(1).replace("\\\n", " ")
                  ).strip()[:500]

              block_m = re.search(
                  r'define Package/[^\n]+\n(.*?)^endef',
                  text, re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL
              )
              if block_m:
                  block = block_m.group(1)
                  d = re.search(
                      r'DESCRIPTION\s*:?=\s*(.+?)(?=\n\s*[A-Z]|\Z)', block, re.DOTALL
                  )
                  if d:
                      desc = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", d.group(1).replace("\\\n", " ")).strip()
                      if desc:
                          fields.setdefault("DESCRIPTION", desc[:500])
              return fields

          def extract_readme(pkg_dir):
              for name in ["README.md", "README", "README.txt"]:
                  p = os.path.join(pkg_dir, name)
                  if os.path.isfile(p):
                      try:
                          content = open(p, encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").read().strip()
                          if len(content) > 50:
                              return content
                      except Exception:
                          pass
              return None

          toc_lines  = []
          total_pkgs = 0

          for cat_path in sorted(glob.glob(f"{REPO}/package/*")):
              if not os.path.isdir(cat_path):
                  continue
              category = os.path.basename(cat_path)
              entries  = []

              for pkg_path in sorted(glob.glob(f"{cat_path}/*")):
                  if not os.path.isdir(pkg_path):
                      continue
                  pkg_name = os.path.basename(pkg_path)
                  meta     = extract_makefile_meta(os.path.join(pkg_path, "Makefile"))
                  readme   = extract_readme(pkg_path)
                  if not meta and not readme:
                      continue
                  entries.append({"name": pkg_name, "meta": meta, "readme": readme})

              if not entries:
                  continue

              total_pkgs += len(entries)
              cat_src_url = f"{REPO_URL}/tree/master/package/{category}"
              outfile     = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f"openwrt-buildroot-{category}.md")

              with open(outfile, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                  f.write(f"# OpenWrt Buildroot: `{category}` packages\n\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Source:** {cat_src_url}\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Generated:** {TS} from commit `{COMMIT}`\n\n---\n\n")

                  for e in entries:
                      m = e["meta"]
                      f.write(f"## `{e['name']}`\n\n")
                      if m.get("DESCRIPTION"):
                          f.write(f"{m['DESCRIPTION']}\n\n")
                      rows = []
                      if m.get("PKG_VERSION"):    rows.append(f"| Version | {m['PKG_VERSION']} |")
                      if m.get("PKG_LICENSE"):    rows.append(f"| License | {m['PKG_LICENSE']} |")
                      if m.get("PKG_MAINTAINER"): rows.append(f"| Maintainer | {m['PKG_MAINTAINER']} |")
                      if m.get("PKG_SOURCE_URL"): rows.append(f"| Source URL | {m['PKG_SOURCE_URL'][:120]} |")
                      if rows:
                          f.write("| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n" + "\n".join(rows) + "\n\n")
                      if e["readme"]:
                          content   = e["readme"]
                          truncated = len(content) > 2000
                          f.write("**README:**\n\n")
                          f.write(content[:2000])
                          if truncated:
                              pkg_url = f"{REPO_URL}/tree/master/package/{category}/{e['name']}"
                              f.write(f"\n\n> *(README truncated — [view full file]({pkg_url}))*")
                          f.write("\n\n")
                      pkg_url = f"{REPO_URL}/tree/master/package/{category}/{e['name']}"
                      f.write(f"> Source: {pkg_url}\n\n---\n\n")

              toc_lines.append(
                  f"- [openwrt-buildroot-{category}.md]"
                  f"(/openwrt-buildroot-docs/openwrt-buildroot-{category}.md): "
                  f"OpenWrt buildroot `{category}` — {len(entries)} packages"
              )
              print(f"  {category}: {len(entries)} packages")

          # ── include/*.mk header comments ──────────────────────────────────────
          mk_entries = []
          for mk_file in sorted(glob.glob(f"{REPO}/include/*.mk")):
              try:
                  text = open(mk_file, encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").read()
              except Exception:
                  continue
              fname_mk = os.path.basename(mk_file)
              comments = []
              for line in text.splitlines()[:100]:
                  if line.startswith("#"):
                      cleaned = line.lstrip("# ").strip()
                      if cleaned:
                          comments.append(cleaned)
                  elif comments:
                      break
              if len(comments) >= 2:
                  mk_entries.append((fname_mk, "\n".join(comments)))

          if mk_entries:
              outfile = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "openwrt-buildroot-include-mk.md")
              with open(outfile, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                  f.write("# OpenWrt Buildroot: Build System Include Files\n\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Source:** {REPO_URL}/tree/master/include\n")
                  f.write(f"> **Generated:** {TS} from commit `{COMMIT}`\n\n")
                  f.write("Core build system Makefile fragments.\n\n---\n\n")
                  for fname_mk, doc in mk_entries:
                      f.write(f"## `{fname_mk}`\n\n```\n{doc}\n```\n\n")
                      f.write(f"> Source: {REPO_URL}/blob/master/include/{fname_mk}\n\n---\n\n")
              toc_lines.append(
                  f"- [openwrt-buildroot-include-mk.md]"
                  f"(/openwrt-buildroot-docs/openwrt-buildroot-include-mk.md): "
                  f"Build system .mk files — {len(mk_entries)} documented"
              )

          with open(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "llms.txt"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
              f.write("# OpenWrt Buildroot Documentation Index\n")
              f.write(f"# Source: {REPO_URL} (commit: {COMMIT})\n")
              f.write(f"# Generated: {TS}\n#\n")
              f.write("# Package metadata and README files from the OpenWrt buildroot.\n#\n")
              f.write("## Files in this directory\n\n")
              for line in toc_lines:
                  f.write(line + "\n")

          print(f"\nStep 6 complete: {total_pkgs} packages across {len(toc_lines)} outputs.")
          PYEOF

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 7: Extract curated LuCI application examples
      #
      #  Four apps, each demonstrating a distinct development pattern:
      #    luci-app-example     Hello world / minimum viable app structure
      #    luci-app-commands    HTTP API / controller; popen; URL parsing
      #    luci-app-ddns        Standard config app; rpcd; service state; UCI
      #    luci-app-dockerman   Advanced; UNIX sockets; streaming; multi-file
      #
      #  Files are copied RAW AND UNMODIFIED. Only .uc and .js files are taken.
      #  Internal directory structure is preserved so the file paths give context.
      #
      #  v5 IMPROVEMENT: The llms.txt now includes a file tree view for each app
      #  so an LLM reading only llms.txt can understand the app's structure before
      #  deciding which files to load.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 7: Extract curated LuCI application examples"
        run: |
          SRC="repo-luci/applications"
          OUT="openwrt-examples"
          TS=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          LUCI_URL="https://github.com/openwrt/luci/tree/master/applications"

          cat > "$OUT/llms.txt" << LLMS_EOF
# OpenWrt Curated LuCI Application Examples
# Source: $LUCI_URL
# Generated: $TS from LuCI commit $LUCI_COMMIT
#
# Four apps selected for distinct pedagogical value. Together they cover the
# full range of modern LuCI development patterns. All files are raw upstream
# source code — unmodified, suitable for direct use as coding templates.
#
# Standalone use: this llms.txt plus the app subdirectories are self-contained.
# The file tree below each app shows the structure before reading any source.
#
# Recommended reading order:
#   1. luci-app-example    — baseline structure and ucode<->JS bridge
#   2. luci-app-commands   — HTTP endpoints and controller pattern
#   3. luci-app-ddns       — config management and service state
#   4. luci-app-dockerman  — advanced sockets, streaming, multi-file
#
## Application Files

LLMS_EOF

          for app in luci-app-example luci-app-commands luci-app-ddns luci-app-dockerman; do
            APP_DIR="$SRC/$app"

            if [ ! -d "$APP_DIR" ]; then
              echo "  WARNING: $app not found at $APP_DIR — skipping"
              printf "\n## %s\n# NOTE: Not found in upstream repo at generation time.\n\n" \
                "$app" >> "$OUT/llms.txt"
              continue
            fi

            echo "Extracting $app..."
            mkdir -p "$OUT/$app"

            cd "$SRC"
            find "$app" -type f \( -name "*.uc" -o -name "*.js" \) \
              -exec cp --parents {} "../../$OUT/" \;
            cd ../..

            uc_count=$(find "$OUT/$app" -name "*.uc" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
            js_count=$(find "$OUT/$app" -name "*.js" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)

            case "$app" in
              luci-app-example)
                desc="Hello world / baseline — minimum viable app; ucode<->JS bridge, ACL, UCI form, rpcd scaffolding"
                ;;
              luci-app-commands)
                desc="HTTP API / controller — custom endpoints in ucode/controller/, URL parsing, secure popen execution"
                ;;
              luci-app-ddns)
                desc="Standard config app — service state (init_enabled), rpcd microbus, deep UCI read/write"
                ;;
              luci-app-dockerman)
                desc="Advanced / streaming — UNIX socket communication, HTTP streaming, large JSON, multi-file ucode"
                ;;
            esac

            # Write app section header with description and source link
            {
              printf "\n## %s\n" "$app"
              printf "# Pattern: %s\n" "$desc"
              printf "# Source: %s/%s\n" "$LUCI_URL" "$app"
              printf "# Files: %s .uc (ucode backend), %s .js (JavaScript frontend)\n" \
                "$uc_count" "$js_count"
              printf "#\n"

              # File tree view — helps an LLM understand app structure without
              # reading every file. Shows relative path and file type inline.
              printf "# File tree:\n"
              find "$OUT/$app" -type f \( -name "*.uc" -o -name "*.js" \) | sort | \
              while read -r f; do
                rel="${f#$OUT/$app/}"
                ext="${f##*.}"
                [ "$ext" = "uc" ] && ftype="[ucode]" || ftype="[js]   "
                printf "#   %s  %s\n" "$ftype" "$rel"
              done
              printf "#\n"
            } >> "$OUT/llms.txt"

            # Individual file entries with root-relative links
            while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
              rel="${f#$OUT/}"
              ext="${f##*.}"
              [ "$ext" = "uc" ] && ftype="ucode backend" || ftype="JavaScript frontend"
              echo "- [$rel](/$rel): $app — $ftype" >> "$OUT/llms.txt"
            done < <(find "$OUT/$app" -type f \( -name "*.uc" -o -name "*.js" \) -print0 | sort -z)

            echo "" >> "$OUT/llms.txt"
            echo "  $app: $uc_count .uc, $js_count .js"
          done

          echo "Step 7 complete."

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 8: Cross-reference injection (pure Python, no AI, no cost)
      #
      #  Builds a symbol index from all generated markdown headings, then injects
      #  cross-reference links into body text occurrences of those symbols.
      #
      #  ROOT-RELATIVE PATHS: All links use /folder/file.md paths. These work
      #  correctly for the modular files deployed to GitHub Pages. They also
      #  appear in the complete reference files (Step 11), where they serve as
      #  informational cross-references even though they point to external URLs.
      #
      #  SYMBOL FILTER (new in v5): Only index code-like symbol names.
      #  Common English words (name, type, value, path, etc.) are excluded even
      #  if they appear as headings. Without this filter, thousands of spurious
      #  links would appear throughout wiki documentation. A symbol must also
      #  match one of: camelCase, dotted.name, underscore_style (6+ chars),
      #  or all-caps abbreviation (3-6 chars like UCI, RPC).
      #
      #  INSERTION ALGORITHM: Collect all (start, end, replacement) tuples into
      #  a list, sort by start position, then rebuild the string in one forward
      #  pass using a "last" pointer. This avoids offset tracking bugs that occur
      #  when modifying a string while iterating over match positions.
      #
      #  Runs BEFORE Step 11 so both individual files and complete references
      #  contain the injected links.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 8: Cross-reference injection (code-like symbols only)"
        run: |
          python3 << 'PYEOF'
          import os
          import re
          import glob

          all_md = (
              sorted(glob.glob("ucode-docs/*.md")) +
              sorted(glob.glob("luci-docs/*.md")) +
              sorted(glob.glob("openwrt-wiki-docs/*.md")) +
              sorted(glob.glob("openwrt-buildroot-docs/*.md"))
          )

          print(f"Building symbol index from {len(all_md)} markdown files...")

          # ── Common words to exclude from cross-referencing ─────────────────
          # These words frequently appear as markdown headings but are too generic
          # to be useful as cross-reference targets. Without this exclusion, every
          # occurrence of "name", "type", "path", etc. in all wiki pages would
          # become a hyperlink — cluttering the output with meaningless links.
          COMMON_WORDS = {
              "name", "type", "value", "event", "data", "code", "info", "list",
              "item", "node", "text", "form", "page", "time", "date", "user",
              "host", "port", "path", "file", "mode", "status", "error", "result",
              "state", "flags", "index", "count", "length", "size", "version",
              "base", "init", "load", "open", "read", "write", "close", "send",
              "recv", "bind", "call", "stop", "start", "reset", "clear", "check",
              "parse", "fetch", "apply", "remove", "create", "update", "delete",
              "source", "target", "output", "input", "params", "options", "config",
              "return", "object", "string", "number", "boolean", "array", "table",
              "class", "method", "property", "function", "callback", "promise",
          }

          def is_code_symbol(name):
              """
              Return True only for names that look like code identifiers:
                - camelCase (lowercase start + at least one uppercase)
                - dotted.name (API.method style, min 6 chars)
                - underscore_style (min 7 chars — short ones are too generic)
                - ALL_CAPS abbreviation (3-6 chars, e.g. UCI, RPC, ACL)
              Rejects common English words regardless of the above criteria.
              """
              if name.lower() in COMMON_WORDS:
                  return False
              if len(name) < 5:
                  return False
              # camelCase: starts lowercase, contains at least one uppercase letter
              if re.match(r'^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]+$', name) and any(c.isupper() for c in name):
                  return True
              # Dotted name: module.method or Class.property
              if "." in name and len(name) >= 6:
                  return True
              # Underscore style, long enough to be specific
              if "_" in name and len(name) >= 7:
                  return True
              # All-caps abbreviation used as an API identifier
              if re.match(r'^[A-Z]{3,6}$', name):
                  return True
              return False

          # ── Build symbol index ──────────────────────────────────────────────
          symbol_index = {}

          for fpath in all_md:
              root_url = "/" + fpath.replace(os.sep, "/")
              try:
                  content = open(fpath, encoding="utf-8").read()
              except Exception:
                  continue

              for m in re.finditer(
                  r'^#{2,4}\s+[`"]?([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.]+(?:\(\))?)[`"]?',
                  content, re.MULTILINE
              ):
                  symbol = m.group(1).rstrip("()")
                  if not is_code_symbol(symbol):
                      continue

                  if symbol not in symbol_index:
                      symbol_index[symbol] = root_url
                  else:
                      current      = symbol_index[symbol]
                      this_is_api  = "ucode-docs" in fpath or "luci-docs" in fpath
                      curr_is_wiki = "wiki" in current or "buildroot" in current
                      curr_is_api  = "ucode-docs" in current or "luci-docs" in current
                      if this_is_api and curr_is_wiki:
                          # API reference wins over wiki/buildroot
                          symbol_index[symbol] = root_url
                      elif this_is_api and curr_is_api:
                          # Two API files claim the same symbol — log for visibility
                          print(
                              f"Warning: API symbol collision '{symbol}': "
                              f"{current} vs {root_url} (keeping first)"
                          )

          print(f"Symbol index: {len(symbol_index)} code-like symbols")

          # ── Inject cross-reference links ────────────────────────────────────
          total_injected = 0
          files_modified = 0

          for fpath in all_md:
              this_root_url = "/" + fpath.replace(os.sep, "/")
              try:
                  original = open(fpath, encoding="utf-8").read()
              except Exception:
                  continue

              # Build protected ranges: fenced code, inline code, existing links
              protected = set()
              for fence in re.finditer(r'```.*?```|~~~.*?~~~', original, re.DOTALL):
                  protected.update(range(fence.start(), fence.end()))
              for code in re.finditer(r'`[^`\n]+`', original):
                  protected.update(range(code.start(), code.end()))
              for link in re.finditer(r'\[[^\]]+\]\([^)]+\)', original):
                  protected.update(range(link.start(), link.end()))

              # Collect all insertion positions — process longest symbols first
              # to avoid partial-name collisions (e.g. don't match "uci" inside
              # "uci.get" if "uci.get" is also in the index).
              spans = []

              def overlaps_existing(s, e):
                  for es, ee, _ in spans:
                      if not (e <= es or s >= ee):
                          return True
                  return False

              for symbol, target_url in sorted(symbol_index.items(), key=lambda x: -len(x[0])):
                  if target_url == this_root_url:
                      continue
                  pat = re.compile(rf'\b{re.escape(symbol)}\b(?!\()')
                  for m in pat.finditer(original):
                      s, e = m.start(), m.end()
                      if any(i in protected for i in range(s, e)):
                          continue
                      if overlaps_existing(s, e):
                          continue
                      spans.append((s, e, f"[{symbol}]({target_url})"))

              if not spans:
                  continue

              # Rebuild string in one forward pass using sorted spans.
              # This avoids offset tracking bugs from in-place string modification.
              spans.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
              out  = []
              last = 0
              for s, e, rep in spans:
                  out.append(original[last:s])
                  out.append(rep)
                  last = e
              out.append(original[last:])
              modified = "".join(out)

              with open(fpath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                  f.write(modified)
              files_modified += 1
              total_injected += len(spans)

          print(
              f"Step 8 complete: {total_injected} cross-references "
              f"injected across {files_modified} files."
          )
          PYEOF

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 9: Deprecation checker (pure Python, no AI, no cost)
      #
      #  Finds @deprecated symbols in ucode/LuCI API docs (rendered as
      #  "**Deprecated**" by jsdoc-to-markdown) then injects GitHub-flavored
      #  warning callouts into any wiki page referencing those symbols.
      #
      #  WINDOW SIZE: 1500 chars (v4 used 300 — too small). The **Deprecated**
      #  marker may appear after a full description, parameter list, and return
      #  docs, easily exceeding 300 characters from the heading.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 9: Deprecation checker"
        run: |
          python3 << 'PYEOF'
          import os
          import re
          import glob

          deprecated = {}

          for fpath in sorted(glob.glob("ucode-docs/*.md") + glob.glob("luci-docs/*.md")):
              root_url = "/" + fpath.replace(os.sep, "/")
              try:
                  content = open(fpath, encoding="utf-8").read()
              except Exception:
                  continue
              for m in re.finditer(
                  r'^(#{2,4})\s+[`"]?([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.]+)[`"]?',
                  content, re.MULTILINE
              ):
                  symbol = m.group(2).rstrip("()")
                  # 1500-char window: **Deprecated** may appear well after the heading
                  window = content[m.end():m.end() + 1500]
                  if re.search(r'\*\*Deprecated\*\*', window, re.IGNORECASE):
                      deprecated[symbol] = root_url
                      print(f"  Deprecated: {symbol} (in {os.path.basename(fpath)})")

          if not deprecated:
              print("No deprecated symbols found in API docs.")
          else:
              print(f"Found {len(deprecated)} deprecated symbols.")

          injected = 0
          for fpath in sorted(glob.glob("openwrt-wiki-docs/*.md")):
              try:
                  content = open(fpath, encoding="utf-8").read()
              except Exception:
                  continue

              warnings = [
                  (sym, url) for sym, url in deprecated.items()
                  if re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(sym)}\b', content)
              ]
              if not warnings:
                  continue

              callout = ["> [!WARNING]"]
              for sym, url in warnings:
                  callout.append(
                      f"> This page references `{sym}`, which is marked as "
                      f"**deprecated** in the official API documentation. "
                      f"See [{sym}]({url}) for the current approach."
                  )
              callout_text = "\n".join(callout) + "\n\n"

              pos = content.find("\n\n---\n\n")
              if pos != -1:
                  pos += len("\n\n---\n\n")
                  content = content[:pos] + callout_text + content[pos:]
              else:
                  pos = content.find("\n\n")
                  if pos != -1:
                      content = content[:pos + 2] + callout_text + content[pos + 2:]

              with open(fpath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                  f.write(content)
              injected += 1
              print(f"  Warning injected: {os.path.basename(fpath)}")

          print(f"Step 9 complete: {injected} wiki pages received deprecation warnings.")
          PYEOF

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 10: AI module summaries via GitHub Models
      #
      #  MODEL: openai/gpt-4o-mini (provider prefix required — without it the
      #         request returns a 404 model-not-found error)
      #  ENDPOINT: https://models.github.ai/inference/chat/completions
      #            (The azure endpoint used in v4 is deprecated and no longer works)
      #  AUTH: $GITHUB_TOKEN — available automatically in Actions, no extra secrets
      #
      #  BUDGET: Hard cap 40 files. All ucode modules processed first (higher
      #  value — newer paradigm, less external documentation). Remaining budget
      #  goes to LuCI API files. Idempotent: files already containing
      #  **Summary:** are skipped, preserving summaries across monthly runs.
      #
      #  API RESPONSE: Always use .get() — never direct key access. The API may
      #  return unexpected structures on errors; direct access crashes the step.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 10: AI module summaries via GitHub Models (openai/gpt-4o-mini)"
        if: ${{ github.event.inputs.skip_ai != 'true' }}
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          python3 << 'PYEOF'
          import os
          import time
          import glob
          import requests

          TOKEN = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN")
          if not TOKEN:
              print("GITHUB_TOKEN unavailable — skipping AI summarization.")
              exit(0)

          # IMPORTANT: Use models.github.ai, NOT models.inference.ai.azure.com
          # The azure endpoint is deprecated. The model name requires the
          # provider prefix (openai/) — without it you get a 404.
          ENDPOINT  = "https://models.github.ai/inference/chat/completions"
          MODEL     = "openai/gpt-4o-mini"
          MAX_FILES = 40
          DELAY     = 1.5

          SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
              "You are a technical documentation assistant. "
              "Given an API module reference, write a 2-3 sentence plain-English "
              "summary of what this module does and when a developer would use it. "
              "Be concrete and specific. "
              "Output ONLY the summary text — no headers, no bullets, no markdown. "
              "Maximum 60 words."
          )

          def summarize(content):
              """
              Call openai/gpt-4o-mini with the first 4000 chars of a file.
              Returns: summary string, "SKIP" on non-fatal error, None on rate limit.
              """
              headers = {
                  "Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}",
                  "Content-Type": "application/json",
              }
              body = {
                  "model": MODEL,
                  "messages": [
                      {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
                      {"role": "user",   "content": content[:4000]},
                  ],
                  "max_tokens": 120,
                  "temperature": 0.3,
              }
              try:
                  r = requests.post(ENDPOINT, headers=headers, json=body, timeout=30)
                  if r.status_code == 429:
                      print("  HTTP 429 — rate limit reached. Stopping AI step.")
                      return None
                  r.raise_for_status()
                  # Safe access — never assume the response has the expected shape.
                  # Unexpected structures (on partial errors) would crash direct access.
                  resp_json = r.json()
                  choices = resp_json.get("choices") or []
                  if not choices:
                      print("  API returned empty choices — skipping this file")
                      return "SKIP"
                  message = choices[0].get("message", {})
                  text    = message.get("content", "")
                  return text.strip() if text.strip() else "SKIP"
              except Exception as e:
                  print(f"  API error ({e}) — skipping this file")
                  return "SKIP"

          def inject_summary(fpath, summary):
              """Inject **Summary:** block after the first --- metadata separator."""
              try:
                  content = open(fpath, encoding="utf-8").read()
              except Exception:
                  return
              if "**Summary:**" in content:
                  return
              block = f"\n**Summary:** {summary}\n"
              pos = content.find("\n\n---\n\n")
              if pos != -1:
                  content = content[:pos] + block + content[pos:]
              else:
                  pos = content.find("\n\n")
                  if pos != -1:
                      content = content[:pos + 2] + block + content[pos + 2:]
              with open(fpath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                  f.write(content)

          # Priority: ALL ucode modules first, then luci API files with remainder
          ucode_files = sorted(glob.glob("ucode-docs/ucode-module-*.md"))
          luci_files  = sorted(glob.glob("luci-docs/luci-api-*.md"))
          queue       = ucode_files + luci_files

          print(f"Queue: {len(ucode_files)} ucode + {len(luci_files)} luci = {len(queue)} total")
          print(f"Hard cap: {MAX_FILES} files (free tier daily budget)")

          processed = 0
          skipped   = 0

          for fpath in queue:
              if processed >= MAX_FILES:
                  print(f"Reached {MAX_FILES}-file cap. Stopping.")
                  break

              try:
                  content = open(fpath, encoding="utf-8").read()
              except Exception:
                  continue

              if "**Summary:**" in content:
                  skipped += 1
                  continue

              result = summarize(content)
              if result is None:
                  break
              if result == "SKIP":
                  continue

              inject_summary(fpath, result)
              processed += 1
              print(f"  [{processed}/{MAX_FILES}] {os.path.basename(fpath)}")
              time.sleep(DELAY)

          print(f"\nStep 10 complete: {processed} summarized, {skipped} already done.")
          PYEOF

      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      #  STEP 11: Assemble single-file complete references
      #
      #  Runs AFTER Steps 8-10 so all enhancements (cross-refs, deprecation
      #  warnings, AI summaries) appear in BOTH the individual modular files AND
      #  the concatenated complete reference files.
      #
      #  STANDALONE USE: Each complete reference file is designed to be useful
      #  without the modular folder being present. The TOC uses in-page <a id>
      #  anchors. Cross-reference links in embedded content may point to
      #  /ucode-docs/*.md URLs — these are informational when reading the
      #  complete reference as a standalone document.
      #
      #  MODULAR USE: Individual files in ucode-docs/, luci-docs/, etc. are also
      #  self-contained with full metadata headers, and cross-reference links
      #  work correctly when deployed to GitHub Pages.
      # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: "Step 11a: Assemble ucode complete reference"
        run: |
          TS=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          {
            echo "# ucode Complete API Reference"
            echo ""
            echo "> **Source:** https://github.com/jow-/ucode (commit: \`$UCODE_COMMIT\`)"
            echo "> **Live docs:** https://ucode.mein.io/"
            echo "> **Generated:** $TS"
            echo "> **Standalone use:** This file is self-contained. TOC links use"
            echo "> in-page anchors. Cross-reference links point to the modular"
            echo "> ucode-docs/ files; the linked content is also embedded in this file."
            echo ""
            echo "ucode is a tiny ECMAScript-like scripting language for OpenWrt system"
            echo "scripting. Provides bindings for ubus, uci, uloop, netlink, and other"
            echo "OpenWrt APIs. Synchronous, procedural, no OOP standard library."
            echo ""
            echo "---"
            echo ""
            echo "## Table of Contents"
            echo ""
            for f in ucode-docs/ucode-tutorial-*.md; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              title=$(grep -m1 "^#" "$f" | sed 's/^#* *//')
              bname=$(basename "$f" .md)
              echo "- [$title](#$bname)"
            done
            for f in ucode-docs/ucode-module-*.md; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              title=$(grep -m1 "^#" "$f" | sed 's/^#* *//')
              bname=$(basename "$f" .md)
              echo "- [$title](#$bname)"
            done
            echo ""
            echo "---"
            echo ""
            for f in ucode-docs/ucode-tutorial-*.md ucode-docs/ucode-module-*.md; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              bname=$(basename "$f" .md)
              echo "<a id=\"$bname\"></a>"
              cat "$f"
              printf "\n\n---\n\n"
            done
          } > ucode-complete-reference.md
          echo "ucode-complete-reference.md: $(wc -l < ucode-complete-reference.md) lines"

      - name: "Step 11b: Assemble LuCI JS API complete reference"
        run: |
          TS=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
          {
            echo "# LuCI JS API Complete Reference"
            echo ""
            echo "> **Source:** https://github.com/openwrt/luci (commit: \`$LUCI_COMMIT\`)"
            echo "> **Live docs:** https://openwrt.github.io/luci/jsapi/"
            echo "> **Generated:** $TS"
            echo "> **Standalone use:** This file is self-contained. All API classes"
            echo "> are embedded below. TOC links use in-page anchors."
            echo ""
            echo "LuCI is the OpenWrt web configuration interface. This document covers"
            echo "its complete client-side JavaScript API."
            echo ""
            echo "---"
            echo ""
            echo "## Table of Contents"
            echo ""
            for f in luci-docs/luci-api-*.md; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              title=$(grep -m1 "
